into: preposition. To or towards the inside of.

ion: noun. An atom or group of atoms bearing an electrical charge, such as the sodium and chlorine atoms in a salt solution.

not: adverb. Negates the meaning of the modified verb.
nut: noun. Any of various hard-shelled seeds or hard, dry fruits from various families of plants.

ort: noun. A fragment; a scrap of leftover food; any remainder; a piece of refuse.
our: determiner. Belonging to us, excluding the person(s) being addressed.
out: adverb. Away from the inside or centre.

pinto: noun. A horse with a patchy coloration that includes a white color.

pirn: noun. A cone-shaped spool or bobbin on which thread or yarn is wound, used most often for weaving.

piu: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Pintupi-Luritja.

point: noun. A small dot or mark.
port: noun. A place on the coast at which ships can shelter, or dock to load and unload cargo or passengers.
pot: noun. A flat - bottomed vessel (usually metal) used for cooking food, possibly excluding saucepans (see usage notes).
pour: verb. To cause (liquid, or liquid-like substance) to flow in a stream, either out of a container or into it.
pout: verb. To push out one's lips; especially, to do so in a gesture of dismay, either serious or playful.

pro: noun. An advantage of something, especially when contrasted with its disadvantages (cons).

punt: noun. A narrow shallow boat, square at both ends, traditionally propelled by a pole.
pur: noun. Dated form of purr (“low murmuring sound as of a cat”).

put: verb. To physically place (sth or sb swh).
putt: noun. The act of tapping a golf ball lightly on a putting green.
putto: noun. A representation, especially in Renaissance or Baroque art, of a small, naked, often winged (usually male) child; a cherub.

rot: verb. To suffer decomposition due to biological action, especially by fungi or bacteria.

rout: noun. A group of people; a crowd, a throng, a troop; in particular, a group of people accompanying or travelling with someone.
ruin: noun. The remains of a destroyed or dilapidated construction, such as a house or castle.
runt: noun. The smallest animal of a litter.
rut: noun. Sexual desire in any of many mammals, often specific to mating season.

tit: noun. A person's breast or nipple.

top: noun. The highest or uppermost part of something.

tor: noun. A craggy outcrop of rock on the summit of a hill, created by the erosion and weathering of rock.

tout: noun. Someone advertising for customers in an aggressive way.
trio: noun. A group of three people or things.
trip: noun. A journey; an excursion or jaunt.
triton: noun. the nucleus of a tritium atom, consisting of a proton and two neutrons.
trop: noun. Alternative form of trope (“cantillation pattern”).
tropin: noun. any of the tropic hormones; one that is secreted by an endocrine gland and targets another such gland
trot: noun. A gait of a person or animal faster than a walk but slower than a run.

tui: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tupuri.

tup: noun. A male sheep, a ram.

tut: interjection. Tut tut; an expression of disapproval.
tutor: noun. One who teaches another (usually called a student, learner, or tutee) in a one-on-one or small-group interaction.
unit: noun. Oneness, singularity, seen as a component of a whole number; a magnitude of one.
unrip: verb. To open by ripping or tearing.
unto: conjunction. Up to the time or degree that; until.
upo: noun. Lagenaria siceraria; a vine grown for its fruit.

pirot: noun. A notional living being used in discussing certain aspects of the philosophy of language.

por: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Portuguese.

ript: verb. simple past and past participle of rip
roun: noun. A speech or conversation (especially rumoured or whispered).
ruption: noun. A breaking or bursting open; breach; rupture.
